content:
Coordinate worker delegation for this round.
Target agents: POOL-0..POOL-4.
Available shapes: 23 shapes available (23 unprocessed).
Batch target: 23 shapes.

OPTIMIZED SHAPE DISTRIBUTION (batch-size=5, 5 workers, 23 total shapes):
POOL-0: shapes [0..5) - InstallationShape (class: Installation), EmissionPointShape (class: EmissionPoint), WithdrawalPointShape (class: WithdrawalPoint), MeasurementPointShape (class: MeasurementPoint) ... (+1 more)
POOL-1: shapes [5..10) - MeasurementInstrumentShape (class: MeasurementInstrument), FilterShape (class: Filter), OperationLocationShape (class: OperationLocation), OperationShape (class: Operation) ... (+1 more)
POOL-2: shapes [10..15) - OperatorShape (class: Operator), ProcessVariableShape (class: ProcessVariable), InstallationPropertyShape (class: InstallationProperty), SectionShape (class: Section) ... (+1 more)
POOL-3: shapes [15..20) - EmissionShape (class: Emission), ObservationResultShape (class: ObservationResult), AbstractEmissionPointShape (class: AbstractEmissionPoint), MeasurementProcedureShape (class: MeasurementProcedure) ... (+1 more)
POOL-4: shapes [20..23) - TransportProcedureShape (class: TransportProcedure), ProcessingProcedureShape (class: ProcessingProcedure), EmissionProcedureShape (class: EmissionProcedure)

Delegate immediately using these assignments.
NOTE: a shape's name is the validation rule's name, not necessarily a class name - never assume it is one. Tell each worker to type instances with the class shown in parentheses after each shape above, not with the shape's own name.

First pass: delegate the next batch and keep instructions concise.

Use SHACL context as source of truth for constraints.

Delegate immediately:
- Call `delegate_tasks` for each worker that should run this round.
- Keep each assignment short and concrete.
- Include target shape(s), target class, and whether to CREATE new instances or FIX existing ones.
- Include unresolved focus node/property details when available from Validation Report.
- Require worker tool call: worker_progress(status, target_shape, target_class, changed_triples_count, validation_result, remaining_issues).
- In every assignment, restate the exact IRI pattern new instances must use: the ontology's default
  prefix (the one shown as `@prefix : <...>` or `@prefix m: <...>` in the Ontology/SHACL context -
  pick ONE and tell every worker to use that SAME one, not a mix), followed by the exact
  PascalCase class local name and a sequential number, e.g. `:MeasurementPoint1`,
  `:MeasurementPoint2`. Workers across different rounds/pools must not invent their own casing or
  numbering convention for the same class.
- Remind workers that predicates/classes must come only from the ontology/SHACL context - never
  invented - and that each triplestore_add call is validated as a standalone Turtle document.

Rules:
- Do not produce long analysis text; prioritize immediate tool calls.
- Skip workers with no actionable assignment.
- Always instruct workers to create instances when assigned target class has no instances.
- Keep workers scoped to assigned shapes/classes only.

{"length":613,"name":"example file","type":"text/plain","content":"MyGlassFactory is a glass factory that produces glass. They have a factory (Operation) that deploys
multiple systems. This Operation is located on a location (platform).

The factory implements a process that is subdivided into multiple steps that describe the process of producing glass. A process
can be subdivided into other processes.

An emission point is a point where emissions are released into the environment. A proces may be implemented by an
emission point (or other system).

Multiple versions of the factory (operation) exist. Versions may have an overlap in systems, processes but also differences."}
